name: Etherisc

issuer.name: Etherisc GmbH + per-product underwriters (e.g., Chaucer Syndicate at Lloyd's)

oracle.name: Chainlink (weather data), flight APIs (FlightAware) for parametric triggers

entity_map.token_standard: ERC-20 (DIP token), product-specific NFTs for policies

summary: Pioneering decentralized insurance protocol focused on parametric products with automated payouts. Active products include flight delay insurance, crop insurance (Kenya — protecting smallholder farmers via ACRE Africa partnership), and natural catastrophe coverage. First widely-deployed example of blockchain enabling micro-insurance in emerging markets where traditional insurance distribution is uneconomic.

sources.title: ACRE Africa partnership: Kenya crop insurance

underlying_asset: Parametric insurance risk: flight delays, crop yields (rainfall index), natural catastrophe triggers.
reserve_composition: Per-product capital pools backed by reinsurance from licensed underwriters.
secondary_market: DIP governance token tradable; policy NFTs not typically resold.
oracle_provider: Chainlink + product-specific oracles (FlightAware for delays, satellite rainfall data for crop).
redemption_mechanism: Parametric — automated payout on oracle trigger (e.g., flight delayed >2 hrs); no claim adjustment.

timeline.event: Etherisc founded; first flight delay product launched
Kenya crop insurance launched with ACRE Africa
Hurricane Hilary parametric payouts processed in Mexico (pilot)
